messages.content: Hello! How can I assist you today?

i need an vba code: i have Sheet2(Licente) and Sheet3(System). When i select an entire row on Sheet2(Licente), i want to search for cells in that row that contain inside an x inside  and display number total of that cell in cell a1 Sheet2(Licente),after that search  in Sheet3(System) first value of row selected on Sheet2(Licente), for multiple value on same column, only one wil be numbered , count total cells found with that value ,compare that value with value from Sheet2(Licente) cell a1  and if number is equal msg box "number ok" if number is different msg box"number is different",  if differenced found copy values from Sheet3(System) in Sheet2(Licente) on exactly same cells, and colour that cells in blue ,after that colour cells from selected row on  Sheet2(Licente) with an mark x inside with green if value C cell from  Sheet2(Licente) is DA and with red if value C cell from  Sheet2(Licente) is NU ,also same colour for first cel in selected row on Sheet2(Licente) for this values, also  for cell C from selected row  Sheet2(Licente) same colours depending if value is DA or NU  , if cells value in Sheet2(Licente) is updated , keep that value as final ,same for colour, and all cells that not have an x mark or colour blue must be white

To achieve the functionality you described, we have to break it down into steps and write the VBA code accordingly. This code will be quite lengthy due to the complexity of the tasks you want to automate, including marking, searching, comparing, and updating across sheets. Below is an example VBA that addresses your requirements:

```vba
Sub CompareAndHighlight()
    Dim wsLicente As Worksheet, wsSystem As Worksheet
    Dim selectedRow As Range
    Dim cell As Range, searchCell As Range
    Dim countX As Long, totalFound As Long
    Dim firstValue As String, cellValue As String
    Dim matchFound As Boolean
    
    Set wsLicente = ThisWorkbook.Sheets("Licente")
    Set wsSystem = ThisWorkbook.Sheets("System")
    
    ' Assume the user has selected a full row.
    Set selectedRow = Selection.EntireRow
    
    ' Count the cells with "x" in the selectedRow on Licente Sheet
    countX = 0
    For Each cell In selectedRow.Cells
        If InStr(1, cell.Value, "x", vbTextCompare) > 0 Then
            countX = countX + 1
        End If
    Next cell
    
    wsLicente.Range("A1").Value = countX
    
    ' Fetch the first value of the selected row
    firstValue = selectedRow.Cells(1, 1).Value
    
    ' Search for this value in System Sheet
    totalFound = 0
    For Each searchCell In wsSystem.UsedRange
        If searchCell.Value = firstValue Then
            totalFound = totalFound + 1
            ' Only count unique, so exit the loop after finding the first instance
            Exit For
        End If
    Next searchCell
    
    ' Compare and proceed accordingly
    If countX = totalFound Then
        MsgBox "Number OK"
    Else
        MsgBox "Number is different"
        ' More complex logic to copy and match values would be here
    End If
    
    ' Coloring based on the conditions you provided
    For Each cell In selectedRow.Cells
        If InStr(1, cell.Value, "x", vbTextCompare) > 0 Then
            If wsLicente.Cells(cell.Row, "C").Value = "DA" Then
                cell.Interior.Color = RGB(0, 255, 0) ' Green for DA
            ElseIf wsLicente.Cells(cell.Row, "C").Value = "NU" Then
                cell.Interior.Color = RGB(255, 0, 0) ' Red for NU
            End If
        Else
            cell.Interior.Color = RGB(255, 255, 255) ' White for other cells
        End If
    Next cell
    
End Sub
```

Note: This script is a starting point and addresses major requests from your description. However, considering the complexity and specificity of your request (like copying the values to the same exact cells and ensuring only one value per column is considered unique), you might need to adapt and expand this code to fully fit your requirements. Specifically, you'll need to adjust the cell copying logic and ensure unique value counting matches your exact criteria, as those tasks can get quite complex depending on your data structure.

Remember that this macro should be placed in a module inside the Excel VBA editor, and you’ll need to select the entire row on Sheet2 (Licente) before running it. The script assumes that the selection is an entire row and performs operations based on this assumption.

i need an vba code: i have Sheet2(Licente) and Sheet3(System) , when i search for an  values in  Sheet3(System) colour founded values in yellow and copy that value to  Sheet2(Licente) exactly on same row and cells

To accomplish the task of searching for values in "Sheet3 (System)", highlighting those values in yellow, and then copying found values to the corresponding cells (same row and cell) in "Sheet2 (Licente)", you can use the following VBA code. This example assumes you are looking for a specific value that can be adjusted in the code. If the value you're searching for needs to be dynamic or come from a user input, you would need to modify the script to accommodate that.

```vba
Sub SearchAndCopyValues()

    Dim wsSystem As Worksheet
    Dim wsLicente As Worksheet
    Dim lastRow As Long, lastColumn As Long
    Dim searchValue As String
    Dim i As Long, j As Long
    Dim cell As Range
    
    ' Initialize worksheets
    Set wsSystem = ThisWorkbook.Sheets("System")
    Set wsLicente = ThisWorkbook.Sheets("Licente")
    
    ' Specify the value to search for
    searchValue = "YourSearchValue" ' Change "YourSearchValue" to the value you're looking for
    
    With wsSystem
        lastRow = .Cells(.Rows.Count, "A").End(xlUp).Row ' Finds the last row in column A with data
        lastColumn = .Cells(1, Columns.Count).End(xlToLeft).Column ' Finds the last column in row 1 with data
        
        For i = 1 To lastRow
            For j = 1 To lastColumn
                Set cell = .Cells(i, j)
                If cell.Value = searchValue Then
                    ' Highlight the found cell in yellow
                    cell.Interior.Color = vbYellow
                    
                    ' Copy the value to the same cell in Sheet2 (Licente)
                    wsLicente.Cells(i, j).Value = cell.Value
                End If
            Next j
        Next i
    End With

    MsgBox "Search and copy operation completed.", vbInformation

End Sub
```

Remember to replace `"YourSearchValue"` with the actual value you want to search for in "Sheet3 (System)". This script goes through each cell in "Sheet3 (System)", compares it with the search value, highlights the cell in yellow if there's a match, and copies the matching value to the corresponding cell location in "Sheet2 (Licente)".

To run this code:

1. Press `Alt + F11` to open the VBA Editor.
2. Go to `Insert > Module` to create a new module.
3. Paste the code snippet into this module.
4. Modify the `searchValue` as needed to match your criteria.
5. Close the VBA Editor and run the macro through the Developer Tab on the Ribbon (you might need to enable the Developer Tab in Excel options if it's not visible).

Keep in mind, this operation is based on an exact match of "searchValue" in cells across the whole "System" sheet. If you need a more complex matching (like partial matches), the condition `If cell.Value = searchValue Then` would need to be adapted.

ok foget about previous code ,i need when i hit an button on Sheet2(Licente) to let me choose what  value to search on  Sheet3(System), search for that value on  Sheet3(System) and colour that cells in blue , copy that cells value on Sheet2(Licente) on same location on sheet ,if is not possible msgbox "nu am reusit" if possible and cells values was copied msgbox"operatie reusita"

To achieve the functionality where clicking a button on Sheet2 ("Licente") allows you to specify a value to search for in Sheet3 ("System"), highlight matching cells in blue, and copy those cells to the corresponding location in Sheet2 ("Licente"), you can follow these instructions to set up a macro with an input dialog for the value.

First, insert a button in Sheet2 ("Licente"):

1. Go to the "Insert" tab on the Excel Ribbon.
2. Click on "Button" under the "Form Controls" section.
3. Draw the button on your sheet.
4. When prompted to assign a macro, click "New".

In the VBA editor, a new module for the Button's Click Event will be created. Replace the generated Sub procedure with the following code:

```vba
Sub Button1_Click()
    Dim wsSystem As Worksheet, wsLicente As Worksheet
    Dim searchValue As String
    Dim lastRow As Long, lastColumn As Long, i As Long, j As Long
    Dim cellFound As Boolean
    
    Set wsSystem = ThisWorkbook.Sheets("System")
    Set wsLicente = ThisWorkbook.Sheets("Licente")
    
    ' Prompt the user to enter the search value
    searchValue = InputBox("Please enter the value to search for in Sheet3 (System):", "Value Input")
    
    If searchValue = "" Then
        MsgBox "No value entered. Operation cancelled.", vbExclamation
        Exit Sub
    End If
    
    With wsSystem
        lastRow = .Cells(.Rows.Count, 1).End(xlUp).Row
        lastColumn = .Cells(1, .Columns.Count).End(xlToLeft).Column
        cellFound = False
        
        For i = 1 To lastRow
            For j = 1 To lastColumn
                If .Cells(i, j).Value = searchValue Then
                    ' Found a match
                    .Cells(i, j).Interior.Color = RGB(0, 0, 255) ' Coloring the cell in blue
                    wsLicente.Cells(i, j).Value = .Cells(i, j).Value ' Copying value to Licente sheet
                    cellFound = True
                End If
            Next j
        Next i
    End With
    
    If cellFound Then
        MsgBox "Operatie reusita", vbInformation
    Else
        MsgBox "Nu am reusit", vbCritical
    End If
End Sub
```

This Macro:

1. Prompts you to enter a search value through an input box.
2. Searches for that value in Sheet3 ("System").
3. If matches are found:
    - Highlights matching cells in blue in Sheet3 ("System").
    - Copies these values to the corresponding locations in Sheet2 ("Licente").
    - Displays a message box "Operatie reusita" indicating success.
4. If no matches are found, it shows a message box "Nu am reusit".

Remember to save your work and ensure macros are enabled in your Excel settings to test this functionality.
vlue to be entered is string value

Given the clarification that the value to be entered is a string, the provided VBA code works well with string values. When you use the `InputBox` function to prompt the user for input, the returned value is treated as a string by default in VBA, which suits your requirement. 

Here is a slightly revised version of the previous code with additional comments for clarity, ensuring it accommodates string search appropriately:

```vba
Sub Button1_Click()
    Dim wsSystem As Worksheet, wsLicente As Worksheet
    Dim searchValue As String
    Dim lastRow As Long, lastColumn As Long, i As Long, j As Long
    Dim cellFound As Boolean
    
    ' Referencing the worksheets
    Set wsSystem = ThisWorkbook.Sheets("System")
    Set wsLicente = ThisWorkbook.Sheets("Licente")
    
    ' Prompting the user to enter the search value
    searchValue = InputBox("Please enter the string value to search for in Sheet3 (System):", "Value Input")
    
    ' Exit if no value is entered
    If searchValue = "" Then
        MsgBox "No value entered. Operation cancelled.", vbExclamation
        Exit Sub
    End If
    
    ' Initializing search
    With wsSystem
        ' Determining the last row and column with data to set the search range
        lastRow = .Cells(.Rows.Count, 1).End(xlUp).Row
        lastColumn = .Cells(1, .Columns.Count).End(xlToLeft).Column
        cellFound = False ' Flag to track if any cell is found
        
        ' Loop through each cell in the search range
        For i = 1 To lastRow
            For j = 1 To lastColumn
                If .Cells(i, j).Value = searchValue Then
                    ' Cell with matching value found
                    
                    ' Highlight the matched cell in blue on System Sheet
                    .Cells(i, j).Interior.Color = RGB(0, 0, 255)
                    
                    ' Copy the found value to the corresponding cell in Licente sheet
                    wsLicente.Cells(i, j).Value = .Cells(i, j).Value
                    
                    ' Set flag to true as we found at least one match
                    cellFound = True
                End If
            Next j
        Next i
    End With
    
    ' Check the cellFound flag to determine the outcome
    If cellFound Then
        ' If at least one cell is found and copied
        MsgBox "Operatie reusita", vbInformation
    Else
        ' If no cells are found with the input value
        MsgBox "Nu am reusit", vbCritical
    End If
End Sub
```

This code works effectively for string values (words, alphanumeric combinations, etc.) input by the user through the `InputBox`. The search is case-sensitive by default. If you need case-insensitive search, you might consider converting both the `searchValue` and the cell content to the same case (using `UCase` or `LCase`) before comparison:

```vba
If UCase(.Cells(i, j).Value) = UCase(searchValue) Then
```

Replace the comparison line with the above code snippet for a case-insensitive search, ensuring it accurately matches your needs for searching string values in your sheets.
no matter want value i search i get message with nu am reusit

If you're consistently receiving the "Nu am reusit" message regardless of the value you search for, there might be a few reasons why the code isn't finding a match. Let's troubleshoot some common issues and make a slight adjustment to try and solve the problem:

1. **Exact Match Requirement**: The code looks for an exact match between the `searchValue` and cell values in "Sheet3 (System)". If there are leading/trailing spaces or differences in case sensitivity, it might not find a match. Consider using `Trim()` to remove leading and trailing spaces, and `UCase()` (or `LCase()`) for case-insensitive comparison.

2. **Data Type Mismatch**: If the cells contain numeric values or are formatted differently (e.g., as dates), you might want to convert them to strings before comparison.

3. **Sheet Names and Workbook Reference**: Ensure that the sheet names used in the code exactly match the names in your workbook, and you're working in the correct workbook.

Here's an adjusted version of the code to incorporate case-insensitive search and trimming spaces:

```vba
Sub Button1_Click()
    Dim wsSystem As Worksheet, wsLicente As Worksheet
    Dim searchValue As String
    Dim lastRow As Long, lastColumn As Long, i As Long, j As Long
    Dim cellFound As Boolean

    Set wsSystem = ThisWorkbook.Sheets("System")
    Set wsLicente = ThisWorkbook.Sheets("Licente")

    searchValue = InputBox("Please enter the string value to search for in Sheet3 (System):", "Value Input")
    If searchValue = "" Then
        MsgBox "No value entered. Operation cancelled.", vbExclamation
        Exit Sub
    End If

    searchValue = UCase(Trim(searchValue)) ' Convert to uppercase and trim spaces for comparison

    With wsSystem
        lastRow = .Cells(.Rows.Count, 1).End(xlUp).Row
        lastColumn = .Cells(1, .Columns.Count).End(xlToLeft).Column
        cellFound = False

        For i = 1 To lastRow
            For j = 1 To lastColumn
                ' Convert to uppercase and trim spaces for comparison here as well
                If UCase(Trim(.Cells(i, j).Text)) = searchValue Then
                    .Cells(i, j).Interior.Color = RGB(0, 0, 255)
                    wsLicente.Cells(i, j).Value = .Cells(i, j).Value
                    cellFound = True
                End If
            Next j
        Next i
    End With

    If cellFound Then
        MsgBox "Operatie reusita", vbInformation
    Else
        MsgBox "Nu am reusit", vbCritical
    End If
End Sub
```

This code treats any input and cell content in a case-insensitive manner and ensures any leading or trailing spaces do not affect the search. If this adjusted approach still does not resolve the issue, ensure:

- The data in "Sheet3 (System)" you're searching for exists and matches the entered value considering the adjusted string treatments.
- There are no other conditions (like merged cells, hidden data, or formatting issues) that might interfere with straightforward cell content comparison.

If the problem persists, closely review the data set for any characteristics that might not align with the search logic and adjust accordingly.

ok lets start with simple code . on Sheet2(Licente) i have an button , when i click on him an input box appear with the message input an value, then search that value in Sheet3(System) and msg box value found, value is a string  and does not matter if i type with big lettere or smal letter

To create a more straightforward VBA code that achieves your described functionality with case-insensitive search, follow these steps: 

1. **Create the Button on Sheet2 ("Licente")**:
    - Go to your "Licente" sheet in Excel.
    - Insert a button using the Developer tab. If the Developer tab isn't visible, you can enable it from Excel's options.
    - After inserting the button, assign a new macro to it when prompted.

2. **Write the VBA Code**:
    - The VBA editor should open automatically when you opt to create a new macro. If you're naming the macro or if it asks for a name, you can name it `SearchValue`.
    - In the editor, replace the autogenerated sub procedure with the code provided below.

```vba
Sub SearchValue()
    Dim wsSystem As Worksheet
    Dim searchValue As String
    Dim cell As Range
    Dim found As Boolean
    
    ' Reference to the `System` worksheet
    Set wsSystem = ThisWorkbook.Sheets("System")
    
    ' Prompt for user input
    searchValue = InputBox("Input a value:", "Search in System")
    
    ' Check for empty input
    If searchValue = "" Then
        MsgBox "No value entered. Operation terminated.", vbExclamation
        Exit Sub
    End If

    found = False ' Initialize the found flag to False
    
    ' Convert searchValue to lowercase for case-insensitive comparison
    searchValue = LCase(searchValue)
    
    ' Scan through each cell in the used range of the `System` sheet
    For Each cell In wsSystem.UsedRange
        ' Case-insensitive comparison
        If LCase(cell.Value) = searchValue Then
            found = True ' Set found flag to True if there's a match
            Exit For ' Exit the loop since we found at least one match
        End If
    Next cell
    
    ' Show result based on the search
    If found Then
        MsgBox "Value found", vbInformation
    Else
        MsgBox "Value not found", vbExclamation
    End If
End Sub
```

This code will:
- Get a value from the user through an input box.
- Search for that value in all cells of the "System" sheet, disregarding the case (uppercase or lowercase) of the input or the cell contents.
- Notify the user with a message box stating whether the value was found or not.

To execute this code:
- Click on the button you placed on the "Licente" sheet.
- Input the value you want to search for in the "System" sheet.

This approach uses a simple linear search across the "System" sheet's used range, ensuring each cell's value is compared in a case-insensitive manner by converting both the cell's value and the input value to lowercase before comparison.
ok i need to found not excatly match but some part of value

If you need to find a value in Sheet3 ("System") that contains a part of the string you input, regardless of its position within the cell or its case (uppercase or lowercase), you can modify the comparison part of the code to use the `InStr` function. This function allows you to search for a substring within another string. Here's an updated version of the previous VBA code that matches partial strings in a case-insensitive manner:

```vba
Sub SearchValue()
    Dim wsSystem As Worksheet
    Dim searchValue As String
    Dim cell As Range
    Dim found As Boolean
    
    ' Reference to the `System` worksheet
    Set wsSystem = ThisWorkbook.Sheets("System")
    
    ' Prompt for user input
    searchValue = InputBox("Input a value:", "Search in System")
    
    ' Check for empty input
    If searchValue = "" Then
        MsgBox "No value entered. Operation terminated.", vbExclamation
        Exit Sub
    End If

    found = False ' Initialize the found flag to False
    
    ' Convert searchValue to lowercase for case-insensitive comparison
    searchValue = LCase(searchValue)
    
    ' Scan through each cell in the used range of the `System` sheet
    For Each cell In wsSystem.UsedRange
        ' Use InStr for a case-insensitive partial match search. InStr returns 0 if no match is found
        If InStr(1, LCase(cell.Value2), searchValue, vbTextCompare) > 0 Then
            found = True ' Set found flag to True if there's a match
            Exit For ' Exit the loop since we found at least one match
        End If
    Next cell
    
    ' Show result based on the search
    If found Then
        MsgBox "Value found", vbInformation
    Else
        MsgBox "Value not found", vbExclamation
    End If
End Sub
```

This script will do the following:

1. Prompt you to input a value when you click the button on Sheet2 ("Licente").
2. Search for that input value as a substring within all the cells of Sheet3 ("System") while ignoring case differences.
3. Alert you with a message box stating whether any cell containing the substring was found or not.

Remember to attach this script to the button you've placed on "Licente" for an easy and interactive search process. To attach the script, right-click the button, choose "Assign Macro...", and select `SearchValue` from the list.
very good ,now i want to copy values fond on Sheet3 (System) on Sheet3 (Licente ) on  same place

To extend the functionality of the macro to copy the values found on Sheet3 ("System") to Sheet2 ("Licente") at the same location (i.e., same row and column), you can modify the previous code by adding a line that performs the copy operation when a match is found. Here's how you can do it:

```vba
Sub SearchValueAndCopy()
    Dim wsSystem As Worksheet, wsLicente As Worksheet
    Dim searchValue As String
    Dim cell As Range
    Dim found As Boolean
    
    ' Reference to the worksheets
    Set wsSystem = ThisWorkbook.Sheets("System")
    Set wsLicente = ThisWorkbook.Sheets("Licente")
    
    ' Prompt for user input
    searchValue = InputBox("Input a value:", "Search in System")
    
    ' Check for empty input
    If searchValue = "" Then
        MsgBox "No value entered. Operation terminated.", vbExclamation
        Exit Sub
    End If

    found = False ' Initialize the found flag to False
    
    ' Convert searchValue to lowercase for case-insensitive comparison
    searchValue = LCase(searchValue)
    
    ' Scan through each cell in the used range of the `System` sheet
    For Each cell In wsSystem.UsedRange
        ' Use InStr for a case-insensitive partial match search.
        If InStr(1, LCase(cell.Value2), searchValue, vbTextCompare) > 0 Then
            found = True ' Set found flag to True if there's a match
            
            ' Copy the cell value to the corresponding cell in Licente sheet
            wsLicente.Cells(cell.Row, cell.Column).Value = cell.Value
            
            ' Optionally, exit the For loop if you only want to copy the first found value
            ' Exit For
        End If
    Next cell
    
    ' Show result based on the search and copy operation
    If found Then
        MsgBox "Value(s) found and copied", vbInformation
    Else
        MsgBox "Value not found", vbExclamation
    End If
End Sub
```

This updated macro will:

1. Request a value to search for when you click the button on Sheet2 ("Licente").
2. Search Sheet3 ("System") for cells that contain the input value as a substring, ignoring case differences.
3. Copy any found values to Sheet2 ("Licente"), preserving the same cell position as where the value was found in "System".
4. Notify you with a message box stating whether any cell containing the substring was found and copied or not.

### To Assign the Macro to a Button:

- If you already have a button on Sheet2 ("Licente"):

    - Right-click the button and select "Assign Macro..."
    - Choose `SearchValueAndCopy` from the list and click "OK".

- If you don't have a button yet, insert one using the Developer tab's "Insert" > "Button (Form Control)", then assign this macro to it.

This implementation assumes that the structure (i.e., the number of rows and columns) of Sheet3 ("System") is compatible with Sheet2 ("Licente"), meaning that the cell positions available in "Licente" can accommodate the data being copied from "System".
